headlines.title: Vance says nuclear inspectors ‘absolutely’ will return to Iran under terms to end war
headlines.description: The vice president, in an interview with NBC News, also said text of the memorandum of understanding between the U.S. and Iran won’t be released until Friday.

headlines.title: Trump to face European allies at odds with him over two wars at G7 summit
headlines.description: Trump will touch down in France for the G7 meeting with allies who are at odds with him over a pair of wars.

headlines.title: Gavin Newsom says DOJ is investigating him and his wife and blames Trump
headlines.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom said Monday that he and his wife are being investigated by the Justice Department and accused President Donald Trump of targeting him for political reasons.“Today, my wife & I joined Donald Trump’s hit list.

headlines.title: Vance says nuclear inspectors will return to Iran under terms to end war
headlines.description: Vice President JD Vance spoke with NBC News' Tom Llamas about the announcement of a deal being reached between the U.S. and Iran to end the war in the Middle East. Vance said that nuclear inspectors will be allowed back into Iran under the agreement, as a signing ceremony is set to be held in Switzerland.

headlines.title: Comparing the current Iran peace plan with Obama's 2015 nuclear deal that Trump killed
headlines.description: President Trump says the U.S. and Iran have agreed on a plan to move towards peace, including provisions that would limit Iran's nuclear ambitions. But some experts say the proposal is similar to the 2015 Obama-era deal that Trump killed. NBC News' Andrea Mitchell reports.

headlines.title: Kornacki: American pride at an ‘all-time low’ driven by ‘massive’ partisan divide
headlines.description: The latest NBC News poll shows President Trump’s approval rating hitting a new low. NBC News Chief Data Analyst Steve Kornacki explains the latest data from the poll and what it means for November’s midterms.

headlines.title: U.S. deportation tracker: Counting arrests, detentions and border crossings
headlines.description: President Donald Trump came into office promising the largest mass deportation in U.S history, targeting the more than 10 million unauthorized migrants living in the United States.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: 3 takeaways from our new poll: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail

headlines.title: Track the president's approval rating
headlines.description: Follow President Trump’s approval rating in NBC News polls and see how it compared to former Presidents Biden, Obama, Bush and Clinton. Updated regularly.

headlines.title: Only one Dan Sullivan is left standing in Alaska’s Senate race
headlines.description: The disqualified Senate candidate, who’s a Republican, has 30 days to appeal the decision. Ballots are being printed June 28 for the Aug. 18 primary election.

headlines.title: Vance calls his ‘childless cat ladies’ comment ‘one of the dumbest things I ever said’
headlines.description: Vice President JD Vance now concedes that it was “boneheaded” of him in 2021 to call Kamala Harris and other Democrats “childless cat ladies.”The admission comes in Vance’s forthcoming memoir, “Communion,” which is set to be released Tuesday.“One of the dumbest things I ever said came when I argued that ‘childless cat ladies’ across the Democrat Party were running our country into the ground,” Vance writes in the book, a copy of which was obtained and read by NBC News.
